terms.label: Observe
terms.definition: Inspect a perceivable object, actor, or environmental condition to acquire actionable information.

terms.label: Move
terms.definition: Navigate continuously or discretely between reachable positions while respecting current topology.

terms.label: Interact
terms.definition: Apply a contextual action to an actor, prop, threshold, or world-state control.

terms.label: Take
terms.definition: Move a portable object or capability into player-controlled inventory or state.

terms.label: Place
terms.definition: Assign a carried, created, or moved object to a meaningful world position or target.

terms.label: Combine
terms.definition: Join two known objects, rules, states, or capabilities to produce a distinct consequence.

terms.label: Transform
terms.definition: Change an object, actor, route, or environmental layer between rule-consistent states.

terms.label: Toggle state
terms.definition: Switch an available world-state axis and observe its systemic consequences.

terms.label: Recall
terms.definition: Return, retrieve, or compare previously acquired information, object state, or spatial memory.

terms.label: Ask
terms.definition: Direct a contextual question or topic to an NPC and receive knowledge-gated response.

terms.label: Show
terms.definition: Present evidence, an object, or a changed state to an actor or validating system.

terms.label: Wait
terms.definition: Advance a schedule or condition deliberately while preserving player understanding of the change.

terms.label: Evade
terms.definition: Avoid a readable threat through timing, positioning, concealment, or route choice.

terms.label: Strike
terms.definition: Apply an offensive action whose timing and target are governed by visible encounter rules.

terms.label: Defend
terms.definition: Reduce or redirect a threat through timing, facing, resource use, or environmental cover.

terms.label: Mark
terms.definition: Persist a player-created note, route cue, or hypothesis anchor for later comparison.

governance.changePolicy: Verb additions require runtime semantics, feedback, recovery, and adapter mappings.
